results.test_input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)

I found several relevant recipes from RecipeTin Eats for your search. Here are the top results:

## 1. **Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (Extra Saucy!)**
This is a big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, and lots and lots of sauce – because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce but you still want some to soak your rice! The sauce uses minimum ingredients but still stacks up to real Chinese takeout, with Chinese cooking wine (shaoxing wine) being the restaurant secret that adds depth of flavour.

Link: https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-broccoli-stir-fry/

## 2. **Chicken Fried Rice**
This recipe only calls for 2 cups of cooked rice compared to almost 4 cups of other "stuff" (chicken, veg, bacon). You can serve it as a meal or as a side dish.

Link: https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-fried-rice/

## 3. **Chop Suey (Chicken Stir Fry)**
This is a 5 minute stir fry that starts off by sautéing garlic and onion before adding vegetables in the order in which they cook, with vegetables that take longer to cook going in first and more delicate vegetables like leafy greens going in last, then the sauce is added and simmered for just a minute or two so it thickens, then served immediately over rice to soak up all that tasty sauce!

Link: https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-stir-fry-chop-suey/

All of these recipes feature chicken breast, broccoli, garlic, and are served with rice!


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found several recipes from Panlasang Pinoy that match your search query. Here are the most relevant results:

## Chicken Fried Rice Recipe
This Chicken Fried Rice is a savory dish seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil, with meaty flavor from chicken breast. The recipe includes minced onion and garlic, which give the rice a satisfyingly rich and salty taste.

The recipe calls for 6 ounces of boneless chicken breast that needs to be cut into small pieces. It also includes 1½ cups of frozen mixed vegetables containing carrots, green peas and corn, which are stir-fried for 2 minutes while cooking evenly.

## Chicken and Egg Fried Rice Recipe
Another variation available is the Chicken and Egg Fried Rice, which combines tender marinated chicken, fluffy scrambled eggs, and day-old rice tossed together in a hot pan, ready in under 30 minutes. This one-pan dish is made with diced chicken breast, scrambled eggs broken into pieces, frozen green peas, garlic, green onions, and cold leftover rice.

## Garlic Fried Rice Recipe
There's also a Garlic Fried Rice that is day old rice fried in garlic oil with a bit of salt.

You can find these complete recipes on Panlasang Pinoy's website for detailed instructions and ingredient measurements.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry Recipe

**Recipe Name:** Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!)

**Description:** "Big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, and lots and lots of sauce" featuring a homemade stir fry sauce that aims to replicate takeout quality.

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Key Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken breast (thinly sliced)
- 400g broccoli florets
- 3 tbsp oyster sauce
- 3 tbsp light soy sauce
- 1.5 tbsp Chinese cooking wine (shaoxing wine)
- 2 tsp sesame oil
- 2 tbsp cornflour/cornstarch
- 1.5 tsp baking soda
- Garlic, onion, and white pepper

**Hero Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2020/07/Chicken-Broccoli-Stir-Fry_2.jpg

Links: [{"title":"Chicken Arroz Caldo Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-arroz-caldo-recipe-glutinous-rice-porridge/"},{"title":"Arroz Caldo Recipe","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/arroz-caldo/"},{"title":"Arroz Caldo with Tokwa (Chicken Congee with Fried Tofu) - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/arroz-caldo-with-tokwa/"},{"title":"Chicken Congee Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-congee-recipe/"},{"title":"Yellow Arroz Caldo Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/yellow-arroz-caldo-recipe/"},{"title":"Rotisserie Chicken Arroz Caldo Recipe","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/rotisserie-chicken-arroz-caldo-recipe/"},{"title":"How To Cook Filipino Chicken Arroz Caldo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/how-to-cook-filipino-chicken-arroz-caldo/"},{"title":"Congee Recipes Archives - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/categories/recipes/congee-recipes/"},{"title":"Porridge and Congee Recipes Archives - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/categories/recipes/porridgecongee/"},{"title":"Special Goto Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/special-goto-recipe/"}]

I found several arroz caldo chicken rice congee recipes from Panlasang Pinoy! Here are the key results:

## Main Recipes Found:

1. **Chicken Arroz Caldo Recipe** - This recipe uses 2 types of rice: jasmine and sweet rice (also known as glutinous rice or malagkit). The chicken and rice are simmered for 35 to 50 minutes on the lowest heat, stirring occasionally every 3 to 5 minutes.

2. **Arroz Caldo Recipe** - This version uses 2 lbs chicken cut into serving pieces and 1 ½ cups uncooked rice. The rice cooks for about 30 to 40 minutes after reducing the heat.

3. **Chicken Congee Recipe** - Chicken congee is a type of rice porridge similar to chicken arroz caldo, usually consumed for breakfast and best enjoyed with chopped scallions.

4. **Yellow Arroz Caldo Recipe** - This version uses packaged yellow rice, which is better known as arroz amarillo.

5. **Rotisserie Chicken Arroz Caldo Recipe** - Rotisserie chicken provides a yummy roast chicken flavor that blends well with the ginger.

All recipes include common toppings like boiled eggs, toasted garlic, and chopped green onions (or scallions).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

metric_results.reasoning: The response strongly follows the expiry-prioritization criteria. From the input, the critical items are chicken breast and broccoli (1 day), rice is also urgent/critical at 2 days, and garlic is near-term at 4 days. Every one of these expiring ingredients appears across meals' expiringIngredients arrays, including the most urgent items with earliest expiry. The first meal already features both chicken breast and broccoli, so ordering clearly prioritizes the soonest-expiring ingredients. Coverage is effectively complete, with multiple meals highlighting chicken, broccoli, and rice, and garlic represented in usesIngredients though not always marked as expiringIngredients.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

test_cases.name: Prioritise expiring chicken
test_cases.input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)
test_cases.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
